---
title: 'The kettle'
date: '2017-07-08T07:04:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4817432986881276832'
blogger_filename: '/2017/07/the-kettle.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Writer'
---

<p dir="ltr">A screeching whistle pierces Roland's ear, waking him from another fitful nap. </p>
<p dir="ltr">He stumbles to his feet looking for danger. He sees nothing but a two day old pizza in the box on the wood crates turned makeshift coffee table, a Kbar stuck into the side, and dust coating on the air. The Colt 911 sat fired stripped on the other half of the box... He'd many to finish cleaning that.</p>
<p dir="ltr">The static on the TV is an indication he slept on the remote again. Steam screams from the kettle in the kitchen, he forgot about the coffee he was making. </p>
<p dir="ltr">The once bright red kettle was now a gold bronze, the plastic handle melting into the lid. He must have been really asleep to let it scream this long.</p>
<p dir="ltr">Another day in Paradise, CO.</p>
